I'm hard-blocked and can't act on the briefing — every single tool call in this session is being rejected before it runs, so I can't even read the briefing file. Here's what's happening and how to unblock it:

**The problem:** A `PreToolUse` hook is configured to run `python "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/dispatcher.py" pre` before every tool call. In this session the project directory is `E:\Programming\zugamind-review`, which has no `.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py` — that file lives in the parent setup at `E:\Programming\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py`. Python exits with code 2 when it can't open the script, and exit code 2 is exactly what Claude Code treats as "block this tool call." So the hook fails-closed on **everything** — Read, Bash, PowerShell, Grep, Glob, Write, even ToolSearch. I tried each; all blocked. That also makes it a catch-22: I can't create the missing file, because creating it is itself a tool call.

**Fix (pick one, takes a few seconds in a terminal):**

1. **Quick unblock** — give this repo the dispatcher the hook expects:
   ```powershell
   New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks
   Copy-Item E:\Programming\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py
   ```
2. **Proper fix** — in whichever settings file defines the hook (most likely `E:\Programming\.claude\settings.json`, since `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` points there), change the hook command from `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/dispatcher.py` to the absolute path `E:/Programming/.claude/hooks/dispatcher.py`. That makes the hook work in every embedded repo, not just sessions started from `E:\Programming`. (Worth doing anyway — any other studio repo opened as its own project will hit this same wall.)
3. Or start the session from `E:\Programming` instead of the `zugamind-review` subfolder.

I'm hard-blocked before I can even read the briefing: **every tool call in this session is being rejected by a broken PreToolUse hook**, and the hook itself is the only thing I can't route around.

What's happening:

- The hook config runs `python "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/dispatcher.py" pre` before every tool call, with a match-all matcher.
- In this session `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` resolves to `E:\Programming\zugamind-review`, and `E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py` **does not exist** — the real dispatcher lives at `E:\Programming\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py`. This will bite any embedded repo opened as its own project, not just this one.
- Python exits with code 2 when it can't open the script, and exit code 2 from a PreToolUse hook means "block the tool call." I verified the block applies to everything — Read, Bash, PowerShell, Grep, Glob, Write, even ToolSearch — so I can't read the briefing, and I also can't create the missing file to fix it myself. This needs one command from you outside this session.

**Quickest unblock** — paste this into any PowerShell window (it creates a tiny shim that forwards to the real dispatcher, so its relative config lookups keep working; a plain copy could break if dispatcher.py reads files next to itself):

```powershell
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks | Out-Null
@'
import subprocess, sys
r = subprocess.run([sys.executable, r"E:\Programming\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py", *sys.argv[1:]], stdin=sys.stdin)
sys.exit(r.returncode)
'@ | Set-Content E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py
```

No restart needed — the hook re-runs on every tool call, so the moment that file exists this session unblocks.

I'm hard-blocked and can't act on the briefing yet — every single tool call in this session is being rejected before it runs, and the one file that would fix it can't be created because creating it is itself a tool call.

**What's happening:** This project has a PreToolUse hook configured (matching all tools) that runs:

```
python "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/dispatcher.py" pre
```

But `E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py` doesn't exist. When Python can't open a script it exits with code 2, which is exactly the exit code Claude Code interprets as "block this tool call." So Read, Bash, PowerShell, Glob, Write, Agent, even ToolSearch — everything is deadlocked. My guess: `zugamind-review` is a review copy/worktree of the zugamind repo whose `.claude/settings.json` (with the hook config) came along, but the `hooks/` directory didn't — likely gitignored or not copied.

**The fix takes one command in a regular terminal** (outside this session). Either restore the real dispatcher:

```powershell
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks
Copy-Item <path-to-original>\dispatcher.py E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks\
```

(the original probably lives in the main zugamind repo's `.claude\hooks\`), or drop in a no-op stub so tools unblock immediately:

```powershell
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks
Set-Content E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py "import sys; sys.exit(0)"
```

Alternatively, remove the PreToolUse hook entry from whichever settings file defines it (check `E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\settings.json` and `E:\Programming\.claude\settings.json`).

Once any of those is done, tell me to go again and I'll read `zugamind_briefing_bzbby3yp.md` and act on it.

I'm completely blocked and can't act on the briefing — here's what's happening and the one-line fix.

**What's wrong:** Every tool call in this session (Read, Write, Bash, PowerShell, Glob, even ToolSearch) is being denied by a PreToolUse hook configured as:

```
python "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/dispatcher.py" pre
```

Since this session's project dir is `E:\Programming\zugamind-review`, that resolves to `E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py` — which doesn't exist. Python exits with code 2 when it can't open the script, and exit code 2 from a PreToolUse hook means "deny the tool call." So the hook that's supposed to screen my actions is instead vetoing all of them, including the Write that would create the missing file. I have no path around it from inside the session.

**Why it probably broke:** the hook config (likely in the global `E:\Programming\.claude\settings.json`) uses `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR`, which works when you launch Claude Code from `E:\Programming` but resolves to the wrong place when launched from a subdirectory like `zugamind-review` that doesn't carry its own copy of the hooks.

**Fix (run in a terminal, then re-ask me):**

```powershell
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks
Copy-Item E:\Programming\.claude\hooks\dispatcher.py E:\Programming\zugamind-review\.claude\hooks\
```

If the dispatcher doesn't live at that global path, a no-op stub also unblocks things: create the file with just `import sys; sys.exit(0)`.

**Durable fix** (so this doesn't recur in other subrepos): point the hook at the absolute path `E:/Programming/.claude/hooks/dispatcher.py` instead of `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR`, or make the hook command tolerate a missing file. I can do that edit myself once I'm unblocked.

Once the hook resolves, send the briefing request again and I'll read `zugamind_briefing_i3cq1_hu.md` and act on it immediately.
